metric_value: Intel cancelled plans to supply silicon for InfiniBand interface adapters
notes: Announced approximately August 2002; Intel pivoted to PCI Express focus
Launch date was approaching when Intel made its exit announcement

metric_name: Industry consensus on Intel exit impact
metric_value: Many industry observers believe Intel's decision will cause InfiniBand to stall
notes: Aberdeen explicitly disagrees with this consensus view

metric_name: Aberdeen assessment of Intel exit impact for Intel
metric_value: Intel's decision is good for Intel
notes: Aberdeen argues Intel rationally exited a market it could not profitably dominate

metric_name: Aberdeen assessment of Intel exit impact for ecosystem
metric_value: Intel's exit is good for the InfiniBand ecosystem (contrary to other opinions)
notes: Contrarian thesis: removing Intel creates cleaner market for specialized IB silicon vendors

metric_value: InfiniBand initiative will not be damaged by Intel's exit
Intel shifting focus to PCI Express development as primary I/O strategy
notes: Intel's move reflects rational prioritization of its dominant server platform position

metric_value: Specialist silicon vendors positioned to fill gap left by Intel
notes: Aberdeen implies specialized vendors (Mellanox et al) can succeed where Intel stepped back

metric_value: Mellanox Technologies became dominant InfiniBand HCA silicon vendor
notes: Mellanox captured dominant share of IB HCA market through 2000s-2010s

metric_value: NVIDIA acquired Mellanox for $7 billion in 2020 making NVIDIA the InfiniBand market owner
notes: Completion of Aberdeen's prediction arc: InfiniBand became central to AI/HPC market under NVIDIA ownership

metric_name: InfiniBand market size in AI era
metric_value: InfiniBand market estimated at $18.28 billion in 2024 projected to grow at 40% CAGR
notes: Aberdeen's prediction that ecosystem would thrive dramatically validated by AI/HPC explosion

metric_value: PCI Express became universal server bus standard replacing PCI and AGP
notes: Intel's PCIe pivot proved correct; PCIe now dominates server I/O

metric_value: Important next-generation data-center I/O initiative supported by IT industry broadly
notes: Study characterizes IB as a significant industry-wide initiative not just a vendor project
